timeline.label: Division President Global Flexible Packaging to retire; successor appointed effective transition
Post-merger debt offering under combined Amcor-Berry credit structure
Equity offering (424B5) following Berry merger integration
Berry Global merger registration and integration reporting begins
Completed all-stock merger with Berry Global (7.25 Amcor shares per Berry share); created ~$23B packaging leader
Announced Agreement and Plan of Merger with Berry Global Group
S-3ASR shelf registration for ordinary share and debt issuance flexibility
Amcor plc redomicile to Jersey / NYSE listing structure (legacy Bemis combination era)
Bemis acquisition closed — scaled North American flexible packaging footprint

industry.structure: Global flexible and rigid packaging oligopoly: a handful of scaled converters (Amcor-Berry, International Paper, Sealed Air, Sonoco) serve food, beverage, healthcare, and consumer brands with multi-year supply contracts and high switching costs for qualified packaging.
industry.share_shift: Amcor gained share via the April 2025 Berry merger, combining flexible film/tray leadership with Berry's containers, closures, and healthcare packs; independents lose share to scale players that can fund sustainability capex.
industry.trend: Resin/raw-material pass-through, lightweighting, recyclable mono-material films, and healthcare packaging demand; integration risk and leverage overhang after the Berry deal dominate near-term owner narrative.
